patch.target: Oahu has too much to do, not too little—the job is cutting the list before your group chat explodes.
patch.before: Oahu has too much to do, not too little—the job is cutting the list before your group chat explodes.
patch.after: Oahu isn't short on things to do—the trick is cutting the list before your group chat turns into a war zone.
patch.rationale: Humanization axis "Specific human voice" scored 5/10: Has flashes of personality but mostly reads as branded editorial template with 'TRYPS Editorial' byline.

instructions.evidence: Genuine opinions 6/10 — Some opinions surface (Pig and the Lady 'non-negotiable'), but many claims stay safe and brochure-like.

patch.target: Duke’s Waikiki: Always open, reliably good, right on the beach.
patch.before: Duke’s Waikiki: Always open, reliably good, right on the beach.
patch.after: Duke's Waikiki is touristy and I don't care—their mai tais and the sunset table make up for the cruise-ship energy.
patch.rationale: Humanization axis "Genuine opinions" scored 6/10: Some opinions surface (Pig and the Lady 'non-negotiable'), but many claims stay safe and brochure-like.

instructions.evidence: Unexpected phrasings 6/10 — A few fresh bits ('who's on aux,' 'group chat explodes') mixed with generic travel-blog cadence.

patch.target: Build in slack for rest or group drift, overloading afternoons rarely works with six or more schedules.
patch.before: Build in slack for rest or group drift, overloading afternoons rarely works with six or more schedules.
patch.after: Leave afternoon slack—six adults on one schedule will drift, bicker, or nap, usually all three.
patch.rationale: Humanization axis "Unexpected phrasings" scored 6/10: A few fresh bits ('who's on aux,' 'group chat explodes') mixed with generic travel-blog cadence.

instructions.evidence: Quotable sentences 5/10 — A couple of lines have snap ('who's on aux'), but most sentences are functional not memorable.

patch.target: Costs can spiral fast if you don't split smart.
patch.before: Costs can spiral fast if you don't split smart.
patch.after: An Oahu week with six friends costs whatever your worst planner allows—usually double what you agreed to in the group chat.
patch.rationale: Humanization axis "Quotable sentences" scored 5/10: A couple of lines have snap ('who's on aux'), but most sentences are functional not memorable.

instructions.evidence: Intro earns attention 4/10 — Opens with duplicated breadcrumb metadata and a TL;DR before the actual hook; the real opener is buried.

patch.target: Blog Trip Planning 7-Day Oahu Group Trip Itinerary: What Actually Works for Friend Groups Trip Planning 7-Day Oahu Group Trip Itinerary: What Actually Works for Friend Groups Last updated: April 23, 2026 TL;DR: A bulletproof 7-day Oahu group trip itinerary prioritizes accessible, proven spots like Hanauma Bay and Diamond Head while building in downtime to prevent group chat friction.
patch.before: Blog Trip Planning 7-Day Oahu Group Trip Itinerary: What Actually Works for Friend Groups Trip Planning 7-Day Oahu Group Trip Itinerary: What Actually Works for Friend Groups Last updated: April 23, 2026 TL;DR: A bulletproof 7-day Oahu group trip itinerary prioritizes accessible, proven spots like Hanauma Bay and Diamond Head while building in downtime to prevent group chat friction.
patch.after: Not every Oahu itinerary survives the group chat. Here's the 7-day plan that kept eight of us speaking to each other through Day 6.
patch.rationale: Humanization axis "Intro earns attention" scored 4/10: Opens with duplicated breadcrumb metadata and a TL;DR before the actual hook; the real opener is buried.

instructions.evidence: answer_extractability 5/10 — The primary keyword answer is fragmented across multiple sections and buried beneath structural headers like 'What Is Quick answer?' and 'What Is Key takeaways?'. No single 40–60 word self-contained paragraph exists that an AI could lift cleanly. The day-by-day answer is accurate but split across seven separate entries with no synthesizing paragraph an extractor could grab as a standalone block. The TL;DR is close but splits across two sentences and omits Days 3–7 entirely.

patch.target: What does a 7-day Oahu group trip itinerary actually look like, without chaos?
patch.before: What does a 7-day Oahu group trip itinerary actually look like, without chaos?
patch.after: A 7-day Oahu group trip itinerary works best when structured as: Day 1 arrival at Kaimana Beach and Duke's Waikiki; Day 2 Diamond Head sunrise hike ($5/person, pre-book) and Leonard's malasadas; Day 3 Hanauma Bay snorkel ($25/person, book 2 days ahead); Day 4 North Shore car trip with Giovanni's and Matsumoto's; Days 5–7 Kailua kayaking, Bishop Museum, and a soft exit from Ala Moana. Total budget: $1,950–$3,150 per person for four people over seven days.
patch.rationale: Quality axis "answer_extractability" scored 5/10: The primary keyword answer is fragmented across multiple sections and buried beneath structural headers like 'What Is Quick answer?' and 'What Is Key takeaways?'. No single 40–60 word self-contained paragraph exists that an AI could lift cleanly. The day-by-day answer is accurate but split across seven separate entries with no synthesizing paragraph an extractor could grab as a standalone block. The TL;DR is close but splits across two sentences and omits Days 3–7 entirely.

priorIssues.evidence: Person missing recommended: jobTitle, sameAs, hasCredential
FAQPage schema has 9 Questions but page shows ~17 FAQ pairs

No TL;DR block (expected

or paragraph starting with 'TL;DR:')
4/14 H2s are questions (29% — target ≥40%)
No visible 'Last updated' / 'Last reviewed' / 'Next review' stamp on page (schema dateModified alone isn't enough for users or AI)
1345 words is below pillar minimum 1900 (target 2500)
Author has no sameAs URLs (should link LinkedIn + at least one other profile)
Author has no jobTitle or description — credentials not stated
No first-party data signals ("we tested", "our data shows", "we analyzed"). Add at least one concrete observation
Only 3/4 human signals present (author+LinkedIn=false, first-party data=false, original visual=true, 3+ citations=true). High AI-content-flag risk
Human voice vs committee 4/10 — Has flashes of voice ('who's on aux') but mostly reads like SEO template output with SERP-style headings.
Genuine opinions vs safe claims 6/10 — Some opinions exist (Duke's is touristy but fine, Pig and the Lady non-negotiable) but most claims are hedged generalities.
Unexpected phrasings 6/10 — A few fresh bits ('who's on aux,' 'group chat explodes') mixed with generic travel-blog cadence.
Point of view vs both-sides 6/10 — Takes stances in places but FAQ section drifts into neutral survey mode.
Quotable sentences 5/10 — A couple of lines have snap ('who's on aux'), but most sentences are functional not memorable.

specificity 6/10 — The budget section introduces a cost table but the surrounding prose retreats into abstractions — 'costs can spiral,' 'split smart,' 'saving hundreds.' The table itself is specific, but the framing copy fails to anchor the advice in a concrete scenario or named comparison. The sentence flagged floats completely free of any number, property name, or dollar figure, undermining the otherwise data-rich post.
